{"agents":[{"agent_name":"/root","agent_status":"running","last_task_message":"Main thread"},{"agent_name":"/root/core_workflows","agent_status":{"completed":"Read-only trace complete. Current authoritative requirements are now in `PRODUCT.md` and `APPLICATION_COMPLIANCE_AUDIT.md`; `STABILIZATION.md` has been intentionally removed.

## Recommended first production-readiness slice

PAY-001 plus TEST-001: make manual approval payment initialization atomic and document the full journey.

Why first:

- The organizer UI calls `events.approveRegistration` from [event-organize.ts](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/src/app/events/event-organize/event-organize.ts:234).
- The service checks for an existing pending transaction before its transaction, reserves capacity while leaving the registration `PENDING`, then creates Stripe Checkout later in [event-registration.service.ts](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/src/server/effect/rpc/handlers/events/event-registration.service.ts:465).
- Each request creates a fresh transaction ID, and the Stripe idempotency key includes that fresh ID in [stripe-checkout.ts](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/src/server/integrations/stripe-checkout.ts:29). Two concurrent approvals therefore receive different idempotency keys and may create two Checkouts/charges.
- [transactions.ts](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/src/db/schema/transactions.ts:35) has no invariant limiting a registration to one live registration-payment transaction.
- There is no page-backed manual-approval spec or generated guide.

The slice should atomically claim payment initialization before Stripe, make retries reuse one transaction/session identity, reject or reuse concurrent approval attempts, and add:

- a real database concurrency test;
- free and paid application → approval journeys;
- duplicate-approval coverage;
- generated documentation from the same journey;
- Browser verification of participant and organizer states.

SCAN-001 is an equally classified P0 and a much smaller sibling fix: [security-headers.ts](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/src/server/http/security-headers.ts:3) globally sends `camera=()`, while [scanner.component.ts](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/src/app/scanning/scanner/scanner.component.ts:99) calls `QrScanner.start()`. The current scanner specs/docs navigate directly to `/scan/registration/:id`, so they bypass the broken camera entrypoint and cannot catch this.

## Current workflow trace

| Workflow | Current UI → server → data behavior | Material gap |
|---|---|---|
| Event creation/publishing | Template event form calls `events.create`; server requires `events:create`, validates tenant/template/input, and writes event/options/add-ons/questions. Details page calls `submitForReview` and `reviewEvent`; tenant-scoped conditional updates persist lifecycle state. | Product requires negative review to return to `DRAFT`, but schema, RPC, UI, and docs persist `REJECTED`. Event creation also performs its multi-table snapshot through separate writes rather than one transaction. |
| Registration/payment/approval | Registration cards distinguish FCFS, organizer, and application copy. `registerForEvent` enforces authentication, tenant, event status/window, roles, capacity, questions, add-ons, and registration limits. Free registrations confirm; paid registrations reserve capacity and create Stripe Checkout; webhooks confirm or expire them. Applications stay pending without capacity until organizer approval. | Manual approval has the P0 race above. Webhook completion also does not prove the received Checkout session owns the local pending transaction before confirming the registration. |
| Transfer | Participant enters a target email; organizer selects an eligible tenant user. Shared server logic checks status, event time, check-in, tenant membership, roles, and duplicate registration, then updates the existing registration’s `userId`. | This is not only missing paid money movement. It conflicts with the newly accepted transfer model even for unpaid registrations: no link/code, recipient acceptance, current questions, or fresh registration flow; answers and add-ons remain attached to the reassigned row. Paid and discounted transfers are explicitly rejected. |
| Check-in | `/scan` starts the camera and routes exact ticket paths to `registrationScanned`. Server requires current-tenant organizer/helper or `events:organizeAll`. `checkInRegistration` transactionally records buyer/guest progress and increments option counters with idempotent duplicate handling. | Global `camera=()` prevents the entrypoint from working. Existing page-backed tests only exercise the post-scan URL. Accepted add-on redemption/undo is also not modeled or returned by the scan RPC yet. |
| Roles/permissions | Shared `includesPermission` resolves wildcard/dependency semantics for client and server. Admin routes are guarded. Role create/update is tenant-scoped; role assignment validates tenant membership and role ownership, then transactionally replaces the join rows. | Role creation/edit has real page-backed persistence coverage, but existing-user assignment docs/specs only prove that the selector is visible; they never mutate and read back an assignment or cover the read-only unauthorized view. |

## Core documentation gaps

- Event creation documentation clicks into a template, then jumps to a seeded event instead of completing and verifying event creation.
- Event approval documentation actively teaches the now-invalid durable `REJECTED` lifecycle.
- Manual application/approval has no generated journey.
- Scanner documentation bypasses `/scan`, hiding the P0 camera-policy failure.
- Existing-user role assignment has no mutation/readback documentation.
- Current transfer documentation accurately describes the old reassignment boundary, but cannot document the accepted recipient-flow/resale workflow because it does not exist.

No files were edited and no tests were run in this read-only pass.

"},"last_task_message":null},{"agent_name":"/root/docs_coverage","agent_status":{"completed":"Audit complete against current `PRODUCT.md`, `QUALITY.md`, and `APPLICATION_COMPLIANCE_AUDIT.md`. `STABILIZATION.md` is intentionally removed and was not used.

Current inventory: 17 documentation files, 24 baseline product-doc scenarios, plus one Auth0 integration scenario. The suite has useful coverage, but it is not remotely comprehensive enough for “a user of any knowledge.”

| Workflow cluster | Coverage | Main evidence/gap |
|---|---|---|
| Tenant onboarding, privacy acceptance, home tenant | Missing/contradictory | [create-account.doc.ts](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/tests/docs/users/create-account.doc.ts:76) protects immediate tenant joining without the accepted privacy/onboarding/home-tenant workflow. |
| Event discovery and unlisted events | Partial | Free registration starts from the event list, but [unlisted-user.doc.ts](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/tests/docs/events/unlisted-user.doc.ts:7) only describes direct-link behavior without exercising it. |
| Templates and event creation | Good for current simple mode | [templates.doc.ts](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/tests/docs/templates/templates.doc.ts:13) creates a template and event with persistence checks. Advanced mode, conversion warnings, event snapshot independence, and new add-on semantics are absent. |
| Event review/publishing | Invalid against current product decision | [event-approval.doc.ts](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/tests/docs/events/event-approval.doc.ts:59) thoroughly proves the obsolete durable `REJECTED` lifecycle instead of return-to-draft. |
| Free/paid FCFS registration | Strong happy paths | [register.doc.ts](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/tests/docs/events/register.doc.ts:40) covers free/paid registration, required questions, optional add-ons, Stripe, role-ineligible states, and persistence. Guest selection and registration-limit behavior remain weak. |
| Manual approval/application | Missing, P1 | No participant application → organizer approval → free confirmation/paid Checkout documentation exists; already identified at [APPLICATION_COMPLIANCE_AUDIT.md](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/APPLICATION_COMPLIANCE_AUDIT.md:315). |
| Waitlist | Mostly good | Join/leave and persistence are covered, but the guide never clearly explains that waitlist email never reserves capacity or guarantees a spot. |
| Cancellation and transfer/resale | Partial and partly wrong | Paid cancellation is exercised. Unpaid transfer directly reassigns by email, contrary to the accepted recipient link/code and full current-option flow. Paid resale is missing. |
| Ticket and notifications | Partial/missing | QR availability is shown, but ownership/security boundaries are not taught. The guide explicitly says QR email delivery is absent; almost all required lifecycle emails remain unimplemented. |
| QR scanning/check-in | Partial | [event-management.doc.ts](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/tests/docs/events/event-management.doc.ts:288) directly opens `/scan/registration/:id`; it never proves navigation through **Scan**, camera startup, or an actual QR scan. Guest check-in persistence is good. |
| Add-on redemption/undo | Missing | Registration-time optional selection exists; scanned fulfillment, included quantities, redemption, undo, and cancellation/refund boundaries do not. |
| Receipt submission | Missing | Receipts are inserted directly into the database for profile/review docs. No organizer submits one through the UI. |
| Receipt review/reimbursement | Strong but gated | [receipt-review-reimbursement.doc.ts](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/tests/docs/finance/receipt-review-reimbursement.doc.ts:14) exercises approval and reimbursement, but currency is hard-coded EUR and CI filters this scenario. |
| Roles/capabilities | Strong role creation; assignment missing | [roles.doc.ts](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/tests/docs/roles/roles.doc.ts:10) creates a role with readback, but only shows existing-user assignment controls without mutating or reading them back. |
| Tenant branding/settings/legal | Weak | [general-settings.doc.ts](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/tests/docs/admin/general-settings.doc.ts:7) is almost entirely narrative—no save/readback, public legal-page path, privacy re-acceptance, CZK/AUD, or fixed `de-DE` proof. |
| Global administration and Email Outbox | Partial | Tenant list/create/edit is strong. Canonical-root changes, cross-tenant authority/audit, locked-setting override, and Email Outbox are missing. |
| ESNcard | Partial | Baseline docs show a seeded card and invalid input. Live add/refresh/remove exists only as a credentialed functional spec, not generated product documentation. |

Highest-value overall missing guide: manual approval. Add a dedicated `tests/docs/events/manual-approval.doc.ts`, matching `tests/specs/events/manual-approval.spec.ts`, and a deterministic application scenario in `helpers/add-events.ts`; update `helpers/README.md` and `tests/test-inventory.md`.

For the current scanner work, use a dedicated `tests/docs/scanning/check-in.doc.ts` rather than burying more copy in event management. It should:

1. State prerequisites: confirmed organizer/helper or `events:organizeAll`, check-in window, supported secure browser, and camera permission.
2. Navigate through the real **Scan** entry point.
3. Grant camera permission and scan a deterministic QR through fake-camera media in Playwright; direct `/scan/registration/:id` must not count as scanner proof.
4. Show attendee/event/option identity before committing.
5. Exercise buyer plus selected guest check-in, partial later guest arrival, and persisted organizer totals.
6. Rescan and document the explicit already-checked-in result.
7. Cover denied camera with retry guidance, unauthorized/self scan, future event, and non-confirmed registration.
8. Verify the participant ticket remains authenticated and the registration ID is not treated as a bearer credential.
9. Pair durable Playwright with the required real-camera in-app Browser walkthrough, including console/network review.

Exact companion files:

- [event-management.doc.ts](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/tests/docs/events/event-management.doc.ts:288) — retain organizer overview and link to the new guide.
- [scanner.test.ts](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/tests/specs/scanning/scanner.test.ts) — durable behavior and persistence.
- `src/server/http/security-headers.spec.ts` — add header regression coverage.
- [helpers/README.md](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/helpers/README.md:26) — document the deterministic scanner scenario.
- [tests/test-inventory.md](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/tests/test-inventory.md:135) — record the dedicated guide.

Systemic documentation issues to address:

- [tests/README.md](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/tests/README.md:12) lacks an authoring contract for prerequisites, click paths, choices, completion evidence, recovery states, permissions, and accessible screenshots.
- The reporter emits generic `image` alt text and flattens output rather than preserving feature-area grouping: [attachments.ts](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/tests/support/reporters/documentation-reporter/attachments.ts:79), [group-registry.ts](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/tests/support/reporters/documentation-reporter/group-registry.ts:88).
- [generated-docs-source.spec.ts](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/helpers/testing/generated-docs-source.spec.ts:171) locks several superseded behaviors, including immediate tenant joining, durable rejection, “QR email absent,” and simple-mode-only copy.
- CI correction: `--grep-invert "@finance"` excludes three tagged page-backed docs—finance overview, receipt review/reimbursement, and ESNcard management. It does **not** exclude either inclusive-tax-rate scenario, contrary to the current audit wording.

"},"last_task_message":null},{"agent_name":"/root/docs_coverage/workflow_catalog","agent_status":{"completed":"Current-source correction: latest `origin/main` intentionally deleted `STABILIZATION.md`; its guidance was consolidated into `PRODUCT.md`, `QUALITY.md`, and `APPLICATION_COMPLIANCE_AUDIT.md` ([audit lines 13–20](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/APPLICATION_COMPLIANCE_AUDIT.md:13)). The catalog below uses only those current sources.

Abbreviations: `P` = `PRODUCT.md`, `Q` = `QUALITY.md`, `A` = `APPLICATION_COMPLIANCE_AUDIT.md`.

## Cross-persona identity and access

| Workflow | Beginner-facing prerequisites and edge cases to document | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| Enter the correct tenant | Start from a tenant’s domain; explain tenant branding/context, canonical links, and that unknown hosts fail closed instead of guessing. | P:61–68 |
| Browse without an account | Anonymous users may browse eligible listed events, but registration needs an account. Explain why some role-restricted events are not shown. | P:19, 114, 143–150 |
| Create/join a tenant account | User must authenticate, accept the current tenant privacy policy, and answer all required short-text/selection onboarding questions before membership is created. | P:41–56 |
| Re-accept changed policy / complete missing onboarding | A privacy-policy change requires new acceptance; any missing current acceptance/answer interrupts the authenticated user until completed. The admin changing policy must see that consequence. | P:47–56 |
| Understand and change home tenant | First completed membership becomes home tenant; later joins cannot silently replace it; browsing another tenant produces a warning; changing it is an explicit profile action. | P:41–45; A:507–508 |
| Manage notification email | Explain that notification email is user-managed, can differ from login email, and applies across tenants. | P:58–59 |
| Open legal/privacy/terms information | Explain hosted versus external tenant legal pages, policy acceptance versus merely viewing legal text, and that there is no fake generic fallback. | P:47–56, 452, 476, 498 |
| Understand permissions | Every guide should name the required role/capability, distinguish hidden/read-only/forbidden behavior, and explain that the server remains authoritative. | P:347–388; Q:142–145, 359–365 |
| Recover from load/provider failures | Primary pages need explicit error, retry, and “no results versus provider failure” explanations rather than blank states. | A:385–399 |
| Find features through navigation | Every guide must begin from a discoverable click path, not a manually typed hidden route. | P:22; Q:65–66, 229, 351–357 |

## Participant workflows

| Workflow | Beginner-facing prerequisites and edge cases to document | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| Browse event list and details | Listed versus unlisted/direct-link behavior; participant versus organizer listing; role-ineligible direct links show an explicit unavailable state. | P:114, 143–150, 177–179 |
| Choose a registration option | Options are mutually exclusive; participant and organizer/helper options use different wording; role eligibility determines availability. | P:152–179 |
| Register first-come-first-served for free | Account, approved/published event, open window, remaining capacity, eligibility, one active option per event, and configured per-period registration limits. | P:158, 164–193 |
| Register and pay | Explain pending versus successful registration, Stripe Checkout, payment continuation/recovery, expiry cleanup, connected-account payment ownership, and that confirmation/ticket appear only after payment succeeds. | P:181–193, 390–409 |
| Apply for manual approval | Show participant application, pending state, organizer approval, free confirmation or paid Checkout after approval, rejection/negative state, capacity and duplicate-approval handling. This complete journey is currently missing from docs. | P:158–161; A:83–99, 315–328 |
| Add guests | Guests belong to the buyer’s registration, need no separate account/contact initially, consume option capacity, use the option guest-price rule, and support partial arrival. | P:169–171, 251–255 |
| Answer registration questions | Questions belong to one concrete option; advanced-mode “shared” questions are deliberate copies. Explain required-answer validation and that transfer recipients answer the current questions themselves. | P:218–220, 290–299 |
| Select add-ons | Distinguish included quantity, free optional add-on, paid optional quantity, stock, automatic entitlement, and what can or cannot be removed/refunded. | P:222–249 |
| Use an ESNcard discount | Discount shares the option capacity; card identity is global, enablement is tenant-specific, and live add/refresh/remove verification is required. Explain provider outage versus invalid/expired card. | P:257–267; A:199–219 |
| Join/leave a waitlist | Joining is an intentional separate action; it is not automatic, does not reserve a place or Checkout hold, and availability email does not guarantee capacity. | P:269–282 |
| Cancel a registration | Explain tenant default versus option override, cancellation deadline, fee-refund rule, resulting capacity release, Stripe/manual recovery behavior, and add-on refund boundaries. | P:190–198, 245–249, 302–308 |
| Transfer/resell a registration | Existing participant creates link/code; recipient signs in and completes the option’s current price, questions, eligibility, and payment; only then is the original cancelled/refunded. Explain fee-refund/net-zero behavior. | P:284–308 |
| Open ticket/QR | Only confirmed registration owners or authorized organizers can render it while signed in; registration ID is not a bearer credential. | P:401–409 |
| Be checked in with guests | Explain pre-start window, organizer authorization, buyer versus selected guests, partial later arrival, and duplicate scan’s explicit already-checked-in result. | P:310–316 |
| Receive lifecycle emails | Cover confirmed registration/ticket link, waitlist availability, event/registration cancellation, completed transfer, and receipt review; explicitly distinguish out-of-scope email types. | P:419–442 |

## Organizer workflows

| Workflow | Beginner-facing prerequisites and edge cases to document | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| Browse and understand templates | Explain reusable organizational knowledge, public defaults versus private organizer notes/checklists, and how templates avoid starting from scratch. | P:318–345 |
| Create/edit a simple template | Default mode has one organizing and one non-organizing option; cover titles/descriptions, location, roles, price/tax/discount, capacity, windows, questions, and planning notes. | P:200–206, 324–340 |
| Switch simple ↔ advanced | Every mode change requires confirmation; simple→advanced preserves options; advanced→simple requires exactly one organizing and one non-organizing option. Missing categories warn but do not block valid operational events. | P:208–216 |
| Configure advanced options | Individually named arbitrary option list, multiple organizer/helper categories, option-specific questions, role eligibility, prices/windows/capacity. | P:200–220 |
| Configure reusable add-ons | Enable add-ons deliberately, select one or more options, define included and optional quantities, stock, purchase limits, and fulfillment meaning. | P:222–249 |
| Create an event from a template | Explain that the event is a snapshot: it is independently editable and later template changes never rewrite it. | P:101–103, 208–210, 341–345 |
| Create an optionless operational event | Missing organizer/participant categories may warn without blocking; an announcement/operational event may have no internal registration. Distinguish this from out-of-scope private invite-only events. | P:156, 214–216, 507 |
| Configure participant and organizer signup | Cover distinct copy/grouping, simple/advanced mode, roles, windows, capacity, prices, discounts, guests, questions, add-ons, and manual approval. | P:101–103, 152–255 |
| Edit a draft event | Explain material versus minor fields and who may edit; after submission, material changes require returning to draft. | P:123–139 |
| Submit for review | Show draft → pending review, locked material fields, pending feedback, and safe resubmission after return to draft. | P:123–141 |
| Sign up as organizer/helper | Same underlying registration-option model, but distinct organizer/helper language and grouping. | P:175–176 |
| Review pending applications | Authorized organizer approves manual applications; paid approval then starts normal Stripe Checkout. Explain pending/approved/failed and duplicate-action safety. | P:158–161; A:83–99 |
| Manage registrations | View attendees/applications; cancel registrations or add-on units only with the appropriate separate capabilities; explain refund choice and settled/redemeed restrictions. | P:369–375, 245–249 |
| Check attendees in | Camera scanner, authorization, pre-start timing, duplicate scans, buyer/guest quantities, visible identity/status warnings. Current camera policy is a release blocker. | P:310–316; A:64–81 |
| Redeem/undo add-ons | Scan registration, inspect entitlement overview, redeem a quantity, immediately undo, and distinguish checklist fulfillment from versioned legal consent. | P:237–243 |
| Submit receipts | Event-related spending may be submitted before or after an event; explain required event association, upload, totals/tax, review status, and that reimbursement is manually tracked. | P:411–417 |
| Understand archival boundary | Non-personal event history should survive archival, but automatic archival is not implemented/authorized. | P:110, 525–531 |

## Tenant administrator / board workflows

| Workflow | Beginner-facing prerequisites and edge cases to document | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| Review and publish events | Review permission permits approve/publish or return-to-draft with feedback, not event editing by itself. Publishing and listing are separate decisions. | P:135–150 |
| Manage listing visibility | Participant/organizer/both/unlisted combinations; direct links continue to exist for eligible users. | P:143–150 |
| Create/edit/delete tenant roles | Explain stable capability labels/descriptions, default roles, dependency implications, and special-case eligibility through roles rather than flags. | P:347–388 |
| Assign/remove existing users’ roles | Current tenant only; changing assignments is distinct from editing role definitions. Include persisted assignment readback plus unauthorized/read-only path. Current docs do not prove the mutation. | P:377–379; A:330–343 |
| Manage template categories | Category management has a distinct capability from template editing; read-only users must not see dead-end mutation actions. | P:383–386; A:375–383 |
| Manage registrations/cancellations | Explain participant versus admin cancellation, unilateral cancellation capability, optional refund, add-on treatment, and permission boundaries. | P:190–198, 369–371 |
| Configure tenant identity/branding | Domain/root URL, logo, favicon, theme, SEO/identity context, and sender name; clarify which fields tenant admins may edit. | P:444–459 |
| Configure legal/privacy | External links versus hosted text, versioned privacy changes, re-acceptance impact, and no fake legal fallback. | P:47–56, 452, 476 |
| Configure onboarding questions | Only short text and selection-list questions; every current tenant user must complete missing requirements. | P:47–56 |
| Configure registration/payment defaults | Registration limits, enabled complexity, Stripe Connect, cancellation/transfer deadlines, fee refund defaults, and per-option overrides. | P:195–198, 455–459 |
| Configure currency/timezone | Fixed `de-DE` formatting and English content; supported currencies EUR/CZK/AUD; IANA timezone; values lock for tenant admins after event/payment data exists. | P:461–472 |

| Operate notification outbox | Document allow/deny, empty, queued, failed, retry, and stale/sending recovery states for the discoverable global-admin outbox surface. | A:359–365, 436–442 |

## Platform administrator workflows

| Workflow | Beginner-facing prerequisites and edge cases to document | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| List/search/review tenants | Show list → detail navigation and operational context; do not imply tenant membership is required. | P:90–95; Q:212–213 |
| Create/edit tenant | One active primary domain, canonical root, theme, currency/timezone, Stripe configuration, and resulting tenant context. | P:61–68, 444–472; Q:212–213 |
| Change saved host/root URL | Platform-admin-only, validated against primary domain, explicit local runtime exception; never derive outbound links from caller headers. | P:61–68; A:347–357 |
| Perform cross-tenant platform action | Platform authority is independent of tenant roles/membership; every action must be explicit and auditable, never silent impersonation. | P:90–95; A:281–298 |
| Override locked currency/timezone | Post-data override must be deliberate and auditable; recorded monetary currency/event instants remain stable. | P:465–472; A:271–279 |
| Review Email Outbox | Allow/deny, queued/error/retry/stuck states and safe retry semantics. | A:359–365, 436–442 |
| Understand deferred boundaries | Automated custom-domain verification, multi-domain automation, end-user impersonation UI, automatic archival, budgeting/categories, and payout-provider integration are deferred. | A:489–499 |

## Required negative/recovery documentation

Every applicable journey should include these, not only the happy path:

- Wrong tenant/unknown host; unauthenticated; authenticated but onboarding-incomplete.
- Missing capability, read-only surface, direct-route denial, and no dead-end action.
- Role-ineligible event; closed/not-yet-open window; full option; configured registration-limit hit.
- Duplicate registration/application/approval/submission.
- Payment pending, expired, failed, replayed, mismatched, or recovered.
- Waitlist does not reserve capacity.
- Cancellation/transfer deadline and fee-refund differences.
- Checked-in or redeemed-item restrictions.
- Provider unavailable versus empty search/invalid ESNcard.
- Load failure with visible retry.
- Currency/timezone/tenant context on every monetary or scheduling surface.
- Accessible names, keyboard operation, visible status—not color alone.

Evidence: Q:115–145; A:83–135, 385–417.

## Current release-truth gaps that docs must not paper over

- Scanner camera is disabled by the global policy: A:64–81.
- Manual approval has a concurrency/payment race and no complete page-backed doc: A:83–99, 315–328.
- Webhook ownership correlation is insufficient: A:101–117.
- Code still exposes durable `REJECTED`, contrary to return-to-draft product behavior: A:137–150.
- Most required notification producers/templates are absent: A:152–169.
- Paid transfer/resale is not implemented: A:171–197.
- Live ESNcard verification remains release-blocking: A:199–219.
- Finance still renders some tenant money as EUR: A:221–233.
- Required onboarding/home-tenant behavior is unimplemented: A:235–257.
- Fixed locale/tenant timezone behavior is not consistently applied: A:259–279.
- Platform-admin policy exceeds current tenant-bound runtime support: A:281–298.
- CI excludes product-facing finance docs: A:300–313.
- Existing-user role assignment lacks persisted browser/docs proof: A:330–343.
- Advanced registration/add-on fulfillment remains accepted planning, not completed application behavior: A:561–589.

## Documentation-test shape

Each generated guide should contain:

1. Who this is for and the exact required account, tenant, role/capability, fixture/state, and external dependency.
2. A click-by-click path from normal navigation.
3. What each choice means before the user commits it.
4. The visible completion state plus persisted/payment/email side effects.
5. Recovery and critical negative paths.
6. Tenant, privacy, permission, payment, and timing boundaries.
7. Explicit unsupported/deferred behavior.
8. Screenshots where useful, backed by behavior and persistence assertions rather than screenshots alone.

This follows current requirements that documentation is product-first, grouped by feature, generated explicitly, and updated with every user/admin workflow change (Q:149–171), while Browser exploration and Playwright remain complementary rather than substitutes (Q:52–88).

"},"last_task_message":null},{"agent_name":"/root/effect_audit","agent_status":{"completed":"Recommended next slice: **SEC-001 request-body limits (P1)**.

Implement one Effect-native bounded streaming reader and use it before buffering `/rpc` and Stripe webhook bodies.

Key evidence:

- [`app-rpcs.request-handler.ts`](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/src/server/effect/rpc/app-rpcs.request-handler.ts:93) buffers every non-GET RPC request without a limit.
- [`server.ts`](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/src/server.ts:338) permits unauthenticated tenant-resolvable requests to reach that buffering path.
- [`stripe-webhook.web-handler.ts`](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/src/server/http/stripe-webhook.web-handler.ts:261) checks 200 KB only after `arrayBuffer()` has consumed everything.
- Providing `HttpServerRequest.MaxBodySize` alone will not fix this. In the vendored Effect version, ordinary Web-request `text` and `arrayBuffer` reads call the underlying Request directly; `MaxBodySize` is consumed by multipart handling only.
- [`receipt-media.service.ts`](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/src/server/effect/rpc/handlers/finance/receipt-media.service.ts:14) legitimately accepts 20 MiB decoded receipts, requiring over 26.7 MiB once base64-encoded. A 32 MiB RPC envelope cap is therefore a defensible current-product limit.

Implementation shape:

- Stream `HttpServerRequest.stream` through `Stream.runFoldEffect`, stopping as soon as accumulated bytes exceed the route limit.
- Use schema-tagged `RequestBodyTooLargeError` and a typed read error preserving unknown causes with `Schema.Defect()`.
- Fast-reject an above-limit `Content-Length`, but always count actual chunks to catch missing or dishonest headers.
- Map oversize failures to 413.
- Keep Stripe at 200 KiB and use 32 MiB for RPC.
- Optionally lower Bun’s global 128 MiB default to 32 MiB as defense in depth; the route reader remains required.

Required tests:

- Below, exactly at, and above the limit.
- Missing, malformed, and misleadingly low `Content-Length`.
- Declared oversize without consuming the body.
- Stream read failure.
- Oversized Stripe payload never reaches signature verification or persistence.
- Oversized RPC payload never reaches decoding or handlers.

No files were edited and no tests were run for this read-only review.

"},"last_task_message":null},{"agent_name":"/root/neon_lifecycle","agent_status":{"completed":"Read-only diagnosis complete. The “Neon container never stops” issue is real, but the main cause is Playwright/Compose lifecycle—not the Neon TTL.

Current evidence:

- Two Evorto Compose projects exist:
  - Current worktree: `evorto-f907c8db`, with four running services.
  - Orphaned project: `evorto-cf2af2cb`, with only its Neon `db` still running.
- The orphan’s Compose label points to `/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/85ef/evorto`, which is no longer a registered Git worktree and contains only leftover metadata/helper directories.
- Its DB created a new remote Neon branch today with no expiration.
- The current worktree’s branch has the intended 24-hour expiration.
- Neon currently has 12 branches: `main` plus 11 ephemeral branches; only the current branch has `expires_at`.

Why it happens:

1. [playwright.config.ts](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/playwright.config.ts:61) launches foreground Compose but does not configure `gracefulShutdown`. Installed Playwright consequently falls back to `SIGKILL`, preventing Compose from performing container shutdown.

2. [runtime-environment.ts](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/helpers/testing/runtime-environment.ts:78) derives a different Compose project name for every checkout path. Therefore, `bun run docker:stop` only stops the current worktree’s stack; it cannot touch an older or deleted worktree’s project.

3. The TTL is only for the remote Neon branch. It never stops the local Docker container. The expiration worker is deliberately one-shot in [docker-compose.yml](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/docker-compose.yml:27).

4. The orphan DB restarted with a new branch, but its old `db-expiration` container did not rerun. Moreover, [docker-compose.yml](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/docker-compose.yml:47) discards every expiration failure with `|| true`.

Recommended fixes:

1. Add Playwright-owned graceful shutdown:

   ```ts
   gracefulShutdown: {
     signal: 'SIGTERM',
     timeout: 60_000,
   },
   ```

2. Wrap `docker:webserver` in an ownership-aware script that traps `TERM`/`INT` and runs `docker compose down --remove-orphans`. Reused, user-owned stacks should remain untouched.

3. Make runtime preflight list all Evorto Compose projects and warn when a project’s recorded worktree no longer exists.

4. Remove the DB restart policy or replace the one-shot expiration worker with a watcher that handles every new branch ID.

5. Remove `|| true`; implement bounded Effect retries, schema-decode `.branches`, and surface typed failures.

6. Add lifecycle tests proving:
   - Playwright-started stacks stop afterward.
   - Reused stacks remain running.
   - A restarted DB receives a new expiration.
   - Expiration API failures are visible.

For the environment copy, copy only missing secret entries into this worktree’s `.env`. Do not copy `.env.dev`; it contains generated worktree-specific project names and ports.

I did not stop containers, delete branches, expose credential values, or edit files.

Task name: /root/neon_lifecycle
Sender: /root/neon_lifecycle/static_lifecycle
Payload:
Static diagnosis complete. No files, containers, or Neon branches were changed.

The main local-container defect is Playwright teardown, not the Neon TTL.

- Detached startup intentionally persists: `docker:start` and `docker:resume` use `docker compose up ... -d`; only `docker:stop` runs `docker compose down` ([package.json](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/package.json:27)).
- Playwright starts foreground Compose through `docker:webserver`, but config has no `gracefulShutdown` ([playwright.config.ts](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/playwright.config.ts:61)).
- In installed Playwright 1.61.1, absent `gracefulShutdown` causes the graceful path to throw, after which Playwright sends `SIGKILL` to the entire process group ([index.js](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/node_modules/playwright/lib/runner/index.js:842), [processLauncher.js](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/node_modules/playwright-core/lib/server/utils/processLauncher.js:180)).
- Docker Compose only performs attached-stack shutdown when it receives `SIGINT` or `SIGTERM`; a killed Compose process cannot execute that lifecycle. [Docker’s official `compose up` reference](https://docs.docker.com/reference/cli/docker/compose/up/) confirms those signals stop the containers.
- With `reuseExistingServer: true`, a stack that was already serving the URL is intentionally neither started nor owned by Playwright and therefore remains running ([playwright.config.ts](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/playwright.config.ts:63), [index.js](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/node_modules/playwright/lib/runner/index.js:821)).

A second likely source of “it never stops” is checkout isolation:

- Compose project names are derived from the absolute checkout path ([runtime-environment.ts](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/helpers/testing/runtime-environment.ts:70)).
- Current worktree resolves to `evorto-f907c8db`; `/Users/hedde/code/evorto` resolves to `evorto-d8fcbd79`.
- Consequently, `bun run docker:stop` in the main checkout cannot stop the worktree’s Neon container, and vice versa. The existing docs acknowledge that bare Compose commands can point at the wrong project ([helpers/README.md](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/helpers/README.md:91)).
- When copying environment values from main, copy only the missing secret keys. Do not copy `.env.dev`, and avoid carrying `COMPOSE_PROJECT_NAME` or generated ports unless sharing the stack is deliberate.

Remote Neon branch lifetime is separate:

- `db` uses Neon Local with `DELETE_BRANCH=true`; Neon Local’s documented behavior is to create an ephemeral remote branch at container start and delete it when the container stops ([docker-compose.yml](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/docker-compose.yml:2), [official Neon Local documentation](https://github.com/neondatabase-labs/neon_local#ephemeral-database-branches-for-development-and-testing)).
- `db-expiration` is a one-shot container, not a local-container timer ([docker-compose.yml](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/docker-compose.yml:27)).
- It PATCHes the remote branch’s `expires_at` to 24 hours locally ([set-neon-local-branch-expiration.ts](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/helpers/testing/set-neon-local-branch-expiration.ts:140), [runtime-environment.ts](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/helpers/testing/runtime-environment.ts:99)); CI overrides that to four hours ([e2e-baseline.yml](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/.github/workflows/e2e-baseline.yml:24)).
- It never stops the Docker container. A local container can therefore remain running after its remote branch expires.
- All expiration failures are currently discarded by `|| true`, so Compose reports success even when no TTL was installed ([docker-compose.yml](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/docker-compose.yml:44)).
- Because expiration runs once, it cannot protect a different branch created after a later `db` restart; `db` may restart five times on failure ([docker-compose.yml](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/docker-compose.yml:4)).
- There are no unit tests for project-name generation, TTL parsing, metadata decoding, API retry/failure, or Compose/Playwright lifecycle.

Recommended fixes, in order:

1. Add to Playwright webServer:

   ```ts
   gracefulShutdown: {
     signal: 'SIGTERM',
     timeout: 60_000,
   },
   ```

   This preserves `reuseExistingServer` while letting Playwright-owned Compose stacks stop correctly.

2. If stopped containers/networks should also disappear, wrap `docker compose up --build` in an ownership-aware script that traps `TERM`/`INT` and runs `docker compose down --remove-orphans`. It will only run when Playwright actually launched the stack.

3. Add a lifecycle integration check proving a Playwright-started stack is stopped afterward, while a reused stack remains untouched. Extend [runtime-preflight.spec.ts](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/helpers/testing/runtime-preflight.spec.ts:153) beyond its current string-only assertion.

4. Harden remote expiration: remove unconditional `|| true`; use bounded Effect retries/backoff for metadata/API operations, schema-decode `.branches` instead of casting, and fail visibly when the safety TTL cannot be established.

5. Remove `db`’s restart policy or replace the one-shot expiration worker with a metadata watcher. For an ephemeral test database, surfacing failure and restarting the whole lifecycle is safer than restarting outside the TTL hook.

6. Add a read-only `docker:projects` command showing all Evorto Compose project names, plus documentation explaining how to stop an old/deleted-worktree project explicitly.


Only if `docker:ps` shows an already initialized stack mapped to port 4200:

```bash
APP_HOST_PORT=4200 bun run docker:resume
APP_HOST_PORT=4200 bun run docker:ps

NO_WEBSERVER=1 APP_HOST_PORT=4200 bun run test:e2e
NO_WEBSERVER=1 APP_HOST_PORT=4200 bun run test:e2e:docs
```

`APP_HOST_PORT=4200` must prefix every command because each package script regenerates `.env.dev`. `NO_WEBSERVER=1` prevents Playwright from invoking `docker:webserver` and potentially rebuilding/recreating an existing stack.

If existing containers are mapped to `4355`, do not resume them under `4200`; either register the `4355` Auth0 callback and consistently use that port, or explicitly authorize a fresh 4200 stack. A fresh stack uses:

```bash
APP_HOST_PORT=4200 bun run docker:start
```

That command is destructive to this worktree’s Docker database state. Also avoid `docker:start:foreground`, `docker:start:watch`, `docker:stop`, and `db:reset` while preserving state.

Once healthy, open `http://localhost:4200/` in the requested in-app Browser, manually exercise the relevant click paths, and inspect console/network behavior before encoding Playwright tests. An Auth0 “Callback URL mismatch” means the runtime port is wrong, not necessarily an application defect.

Most important findings:

1. There is a guaranteed stale unit assertion. [permissions.spec.ts](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/src/shared/permissions/permissions.spec.ts:45) still expects “Assign user roles (future)” and “not available in the current admin UI,” while [permissions.ts](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/src/shared/permissions/permissions.ts:226) reflects the implemented role-assignment UI. `test:unit` includes this file.

2. Passing tests currently preserve several noncompliant behaviors:
   - Durable `REJECTED` event state.
   - Paid transfer/resale unavailable.
   - QR confirmation email absent.
   - Simple registration configuration as the only mode.
   
   These expectations are explicitly pinned in [generated-docs-source.spec.ts](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/helpers/testing/generated-docs-source.spec.ts:339), so they must change with EVT-001, PROD-001, PROD-002, and advanced registration work.

3. Audit-to-test gaps:
   - SCAN-001: current scanner specs jump directly to `/scan/registration/:id`; camera policy and camera-to-QR navigation are untested.
   - PAY-001/TEST-001: application creation and UI guards have unit coverage, but approval behavior, concurrency, and the complete page/doc journey do not.
   - PAY-002: webhook replay lacks unrelated-session and mismatched metadata/account/amount/currency negatives.
   - SEC-001: no body-size boundary tests.
   - TEST-002: role creation/edit is covered, but existing-user role mutation/readback and unauthorized read-only behavior are not.
   - TEST-004: general settings only persist part of the form; Email Outbox has no Playwright or generated docs.
   - FIN-001/TEN-001: no CZK/AUD receipt or tenant-timezone runtime journey.
   - EFFECT-002 is the single plain `Effect.runPromise` test identified by the audit.

4. CI-001 is valid, but the audit’s exact scope is slightly wrong. The CI command discovers 28 tests versus 31 unfiltered. It excludes:
   - `Manage finances @finance`
   - `Review and reimburse receipts @finance`
   - `Manage ESN discount card @finance`
   
   Inclusive tax-rate docs still run.

5. The standard documentation publish command runs only `docs-baseline`. The real screenshot-backed Auth0 account-creation guide is `docs-integration`, and no CI workflow runs integration projects. Consequently, the normal publishing route cannot publish that complete first-time-user journey.

6. [test-inventory.md](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/tests/test-inventory.md:1) is stale:
   - “Updated” remains 2026-05-21.
   - It claims create-account and Stripe webhook specs skip without credentials, but both now fail fast.
   - It mentions remaining fixmes although none exist.
   - It says Browser/live ESNcard are the only hard blockers, contradicting the compliance audit.

Runnability classification:

- Runnable with the deterministic baseline: scanner policy, body limits, event lifecycle, free manual approval, role assignment, tenant settings/outbox, currency display, permission visibility, error/retry states, and CI gates.
- Credential-dependent but already represented: Auth0 account creation and Stripe-backed paths.
- Genuine external release gate: live ESNcard success/error behavior. This is the sole active skip.
- Not testable end-to-end until implemented: paid resale, onboarding/home tenant, missing notifications, advanced registration configuration, and add-on redemption/cancellation.

Recommended first production slice: SCAN-001. It is P0, bounded, and needs no product decision: permit first-party camera access, add a header regression test, use Browser on `/scan`, add camera/decode/navigation Playwright coverage, and update check-in documentation. Immediately before enabling unit CI, repair the stale role-permission assertion.

The next substantial workflow slice should combine PAY-001 with TEST-001: atomically claim manual approval, prove concurrent approval safety, then add participant application → organizer approval → confirmation/Checkout functional and documentation journeys.

Message Type: MESSAGE
Task name: /root
Sender: /root/playwright_shutdown_test
Payload:

Message Type: FINAL_ANSWER
Task name: /root
Sender: /root/playwright_shutdown_test
Payload:
Minimal, safe change:

```ts
return {
  command: 'bun run docker:webserver',
  gracefulShutdown: { signal: 'SIGTERM', timeout: 60_000 },
  reuseExistingServer: true,
  timeout: 240_000,
  url,
} as const;
```

Add this assertion to the existing Docker startup test in [runtime-preflight.spec.ts](/Users/hedde/.codex/worktrees/b7e9/evorto/helpers/testing/runtime-preflight.spec.ts:160):

```ts
expect(playwrightConfig).toMatch(
  /command: 'bun run docker:webserver',[\s\S]*?gracefulShutdown:\s*\{\s*signal:\s*'SIGTERM',\s*timeout:\s*60_000,?\s*\}/,
);
```

This is preferable to importing the config because doing so requires mandatory environment/process setup. Playwright 1.61.1 confirms SIGTERM is required for Docker shutdown and only terminates a server process it launched; an already-running reused stack remains untouched.

No files or Docker state changed.

# Full Application Compliance Audit

Audit date: 2026-07-09
Baseline: `origin/main` at `9545a2c68d2` (`feat: implement relaunch registration decisions (#83)`)
Scope: static, folder-by-folder review of the application, server/runtime, data layer, shared contracts, tests, CI, and product documentation.

## Outcome

The application has a strong tenant/permission/registration foundation, but it is **not ready to be treated as a full production replacement** against the root product, architecture, and quality documents until the P0/P1 items below are resolved or explicitly re-scoped. The highest risks are QR check-in being disabled by a global security header, payment integrity races/trust gaps, absent product-required notification types and paid resale, and unimplemented onboarding/tenant-runtime behavior.

## Method and constraints

- Read the root product, architecture, and quality documents plus the nearest module guidance.
- Reviewed `src/app`, `src/server`, `src/db`, `src/shared`, `tests`, `helpers`, root tooling, and CI configuration. The source surface includes 439 TypeScript/HTML/SCSS application files.
- Applied the requested Effect, Uncodixfy, and Material 3 reviews. Material findings are adapted to Angular Material and the project’s `--mat-sys-*`/Tailwind bridge; this is not an `@material/web` audit.
- Ran the safe documentation-discovery command: `bun run test:e2e:docs -- --list`. It found 31 docs/setup tests in 19 files, including the `@finance` documents that CI currently filters out.
- Did not start Docker, run destructive database commands, or claim a Browser walkthrough. A Codex in-app Browser walkthrough remains required when UI behavior is in scope and its control transport is healthy.
- The requested Effect skill and its guides now use this repository’s approved `repos/effect` vendor location; no second vendor or symlink is maintained.
- Consolidated the prior stabilization ledger into the root documents, this audit, and the concise `QUALITY.md` manual review queue. The historical ledger is intentionally removed rather than retained as a second release-truth document.

## Severity definitions

| Severity | Meaning                                                                                                                      |
| -------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| P0       | Direct release blocker: can prevent a core workflow or cause immediate payment/security harm.                                |
| P1       | High-risk product, payment, security, or verification failure that must be resolved before a production-replacement release. |
| P2       | Material correctness, UX, resilience, or release-confidence gap; schedule in the next stabilization batch.                   |
| P3       | Important conformance or maintainability cleanup with bounded immediate impact.                                              |

## Findings at a glance

| ID         | Severity | Area               | Short finding                                                                                              |
| ---------- | -------- | ------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| SCAN-001   | P0       | Check-in           | Global `Permissions-Policy` disables the camera used by `/scan`.                                           |
| PAY-001    | P0       | Payments           | Concurrent manual approval can create multiple Checkout sessions for one registration.                     |
| PAY-002    | P1       | Payments           | Webhooks trust session metadata without proving local transaction/session ownership.                       |
| SEC-001    | P1       | Runtime            | RPC and Stripe webhooks buffer unbounded request bodies.                                                   |
| EVT-001    | P1       | Event review       | The application keeps a durable `REJECTED` state despite the accepted return-to-draft lifecycle.           |
| PROD-001   | P1       | Notifications      | Most email notifications required by `PRODUCT.md` have no producer or outbox kind.                         |
| PROD-002   | P1       | Registrations      | Paid transfer/resale remains intentionally unavailable, but is required for the production replacement.    |
| ESN-001    | P1       | ESNcard            | Live external ESNcard add, refresh, and remove verification is required but remains credential-gated.      |
| FIN-001    | P1       | Finance UI         | Receipt amounts are rendered as EUR even when the tenant currency is CZK or AUD.                           |
| ONB-001    | P1       | Tenant onboarding  | Accepted privacy/onboarding/home-tenant rules have no model, form, or server enforcement.                  |
| TEN-001    | P1       | Tenant settings    | Fixed `de-DE` formatting and tenant currency/timezone are not consistently applied at runtime.             |
| ADMIN-001  | P1       | Platform admin     | Platform admins have broad permissions but cannot perform normal tenant actions without tenant membership. |
| CI-001     | P1       | CI/docs            | CI silently excludes all `@finance` generated docs despite those flows being product-facing.               |
| TEST-001   | P1       | Manual approval    | The newly supported application/manual-approval journey has no page-backed spec or generated doc.          |
| TEST-002   | P1       | Roles              | Existing-user role assignment has no mutation/readback browser or docs coverage.                           |
| SEC-002    | P2       | Links              | Approval emails and Stripe return URLs trust caller-provided `Origin`.                                     |
| OPS-001    | P2       | Notifications      | A process crash can leave outbox rows permanently in `sending`.                                            |
| DATA-001   | P2       | Tenancy            | Cross-tenant role/registration tuple integrity is enforced in handlers but not by database constraints.    |
| UX-001     | P2       | Permissions        | Template actions are visible even when the user lacks the corresponding capability.                        |
| UX-002     | P2       | Resilience         | Several core views have no first-load error/retry state.                                                   |
| UX-003     | P2       | Location           | Location-provider/configuration failures are presented as “no results.”                                    |
| UI-001     | P2       | Theming            | The Material/Tailwind semantic success/warning token bridge is incomplete.                                 |
| A11Y-001   | P2       | Accessibility      | Reusable icon selection and several icon-only actions lack keyboard or accessible-name support.            |
| TEST-003   | P1       | Release confidence | CI has E2E and an implicit Docker build, but no branch protection, lint, unit, or Knope quality gate.      |
| TEST-004   | P2       | Coverage           | New tenant operational settings and the global Email Outbox lack durable UI/docs coverage.                 |
| EFFECT-001 | P3       | Contracts          | The tenant settings RPC accepts `defaultLocation` through `Schema.Any`.                                    |
| EFFECT-002 | P3       | Tests              | One server Effect test bypasses the project’s preferred `it.effect` runtime.                               |

## Release blockers and high-risk findings

### SCAN-001 — Global security policy makes QR check-in unusable

**Severity:** P0
**Why it matters:** QR scanning is a core organizer workflow.

**Evidence**

9:The application has a strong tenant/permission/registration foundation, but it is **not ready to be treated as a full production replacement** against the root product, architecture, and quality documents until the P0/P1 items below are resolved or explicitly re-scoped. The highest risks are QR check-in being disabled by a global security header, payment integrity races/trust gaps, absent product-required notification types and paid resale, and unimplemented onboarding/tenant-runtime behavior.
34:| SCAN-001   | P0       | Check-in           | Global `Permissions-Policy` disables the camera used by `/scan`.                                           |
40:| PROD-002   | P1       | Registrations      | Paid transfer/resale remains intentionally unavailable, but is required for the production replacement.    |
64:### SCAN-001 — Global security policy makes QR check-in unusable
174:**Why it matters:** `PRODUCT.md:227-251` requires a transfer/resale flow that
229:- EUR/€ is hard-coded in `src/app/finance/shared/receipt-form/receipt-form-fields.component.html:25-72`, `src/app/finance/receipt-approval-list/receipt-approval-list.component.html:34`, `src/app/finance/receipt-refund-list/receipt-refund-list.component.html:39-45`, `src/app/events/event-organize/event-organize.html:275-277`, and `src/app/profile/user-profile/user-profile.component.ts:213-214`.
381:- `src/app/templates/template-details/template-details.component.html:27-42` always shows Edit/Create event, while `src/app/templates/templates.routes.ts:45-65` guards those routes.
506:  is required before a paid-event production replacement launch.
593:1. **Payment/security containment:** PAY-001, PAY-002, SEC-001, and SCAN-001, with targeted regression tests before any broad refactor.
601:## Validation record

## Decisions recorded

- The listed customer-facing notifications remain launch scope. Render their
  templates with React Email while retaining transactional outbox delivery.
- Paid transfer/resale, including recipient payment and Stripe refund handling,
  is required before a paid-event production replacement launch.
- The first completed tenant membership becomes a user's home tenant. Users can
  change it only through an explicit profile action.
- Production public links use the tenant record's validated canonical root URL;
  development uses an explicit local runtime origin, and only a platform
  administrator may change the saved tenant host or root URL.
- Payments and refunds use the tenant's Stripe Connect account. Evorto attaches
  that account id to the Stripe request and adds only its application fee.
- Waitlist messages are informative and never reserve capacity or hold checkout.
- Cancellation/transfer timing and fee-refund rules default to tenant settings
  and may be overridden per registration option. New tenants default to transfer
  until event start, five-day cancellation, and fee refund enabled.
- Transfer recipients complete the current registration option's full flow and
  eligibility check; they do not inherit price, discount, or answers. Recipient
  confirmation precedes original-registration cancellation/refund.
- A non-fee-refund refund returns the payment less applicable fees so the tenant
  is net zero.
- Tenant currencies are `EUR`, `CZK`, and `AUD`; tenant timezones are IANA
  names with `Europe/Berlin` as the default.
- Every privacy-policy change requires re-acceptance, and required short-text
  or selection-list answers are checked and collected for every tenant user.
- Platform actions have immutable actor, target, action, before/after, reason,
  and timestamp audit entries.
- Live ESNcard add, refresh, and remove verification is required for release.
- Codex in-app Browser walkthroughs remain the requested manual-review tool and
  complement, rather than replace, Playwright coverage.
- The Effect skill bundle uses the repository's `repos/effect` vendor path.
- Templates and events own independent simple/advanced registration
  configuration. Event creation snapshots template configuration; later template
  edits do not modify existing events.
- Simple configuration remains the default. Advanced configuration is an
  arbitrary named option list, with non-blocking warnings when it lacks an
  organizing or non-organizing option. Every mode change requires explicit
  confirmation; returning to simple mode additionally requires exactly one of
  each.
- Add-ons are advanced, reusable registration-option configuration. An add-on
  can attach to multiple options through explicit multi-selection, with included
  entitlement quantity distinct from optional purchase quantity.
- Included add-ons are automatically granted, stock-reserved, and priced into
  the registration option. Optional units remain purchasable independently,
  including alongside an included quantity.
- Every add-on is redeemable from a scanned registration with immediate undo.
  The organizer scan view supplies the overview; organizer/check-in access
  governs redemption.
- Guests remain a capacity-aware registration feature rather than an ordinary
  stock add-on. They retain option-price and partial-check-in behavior.
- Stock is editable for future registrations without rewriting settled
  entitlement records. A separate unilateral-cancellation capability governs
  registration/add-on cancellation and optional refunds; redeemed and included
  units are never refunded as ordinary add-on purchases.

The headline policy decisions are recorded. CI/release enforcement is an
implementation task: TEST-003 records the verified absence of branch protection
and required quality checks.

## Planned registration configuration implementation

This is accepted planning only; no application, schema, API, or UI change has
been made by this audit update.

1. **Model and contracts:** add an event-owned simple/advanced mode, replace the
   two-option-only template write contract with stable option arrays, and model
   add-on attachment included/optional quantities separately. Extend settled
   add-on purchase records with granted, included, redeemed, and cancelled
   quantities. Add the distinct unilateral-cancellation capability.
2. **Server invariants:** preserve template-to-event snapshots, enforce the
   safe advanced-to-simple conversion rule, keep warning-only organizer/
   participant diagnostics, apply tenant and permission checks to every option,
   attachment, redemption, stock, cancellation, and refund write, and perform
   stock/refund transitions atomically against the tenant's Stripe Connect
   account.
3. **Editor composition:** use a page-owned Signal Form with shallow arrays and
   projected partial forms. Simple mode shows its two familiar option sections;
   advanced mode shows individually named options. Add-on editing stays hidden
   until enabled and uses a reusable add-on list plus explicit option
   multi-selection, not deeply nested child form models.
4. **Organizer fulfillment:** extend the scanned-registration response and UI
   with an add-on overview, one-click redeem, immediate undo, and clear
   remaining quantities. Keep guest check-in on its existing typed capacity
   path.
5. **Evidence:** add schema/handler tests for association, fulfillment,
   cancellation, refund, stock, tenancy, and permission invariants; add
   page-backed Playwright and generated documentation for simple/advanced
   editing, event independence, redemption/undo, and authorized cancellation.

## Recommended execution order

1. **Payment/security containment:** PAY-001, PAY-002, SEC-001, and SCAN-001, with targeted regression tests before any broad refactor.
2. **Product-release commitments:** EVT-001, PROD-001, PROD-002, ESN-001,
   FIN-001, ONB-001, TEN-001, ADMIN-001, and manual-approval coverage.
3. **Truthful release evidence:** CI-001, TEST-001 through TEST-003, including
   the required CI/branch-protection work in TEST-003.
4. **Permission and user-state quality:** UX-001 through UX-003, UI-001, A11Y-001, OPS-001, DATA-001, and TEST-004.
5. **Effect cleanup:** EFFECT-001 and EFFECT-002 alongside the closest touched feature work.

## Validation record

- `git fetch --no-tags origin` and rebase completed; audit branch starts from `origin/main` at `9545a2c68d2`.
- `gh api repos/evorto-app/app/branches/main/protection` returned `404 Branch not protected`; repository workflow review found E2E and implicit Docker build coverage but no lint, unit, or Knope/change-file gate.
- `bun run test:e2e:docs -- --list` passed: 31 docs/setup tests in 19 files, with finance docs visible locally.
- No Docker stack, database reset, full Playwright run, or Browser walkthrough was performed for this audit.

payload.message: The Neon sidequest has a concrete root cause: Playwright was killing its foreground Compose process group without giving Compose a termination signal, so the Neon container could survive. I’ve added the ownership-safe fix—only stacks Playwright starts get `SIGTERM`; an already-running stack is still left alone—and I’m validating that alongside the scanner docs.

content.text: The Neon sidequest has a concrete root cause: Playwright was killing its foreground Compose process group without giving Compose a termination signal, so the Neon container could survive. I’ve added the ownership-safe fix—only stacks Playwright starts get `SIGTERM`; an already-running stack is still left alone—and I’m validating that alongside the scanner docs.

---
title: Check in event attendees
---
{% callout type="note" title="Before you start" %}
You need a confirmed organizer/helper registration for the event or the **Organize all events** permission. Check-in is available during the event's check-in window. Use a secure, current browser on a device with a camera.
{% /callout %}

# Check in event attendees

This guide explains the complete check-in flow, including camera access, attendee verification, guests arriving at different times, and duplicate scans.

## Open the scanner

1. Sign in to the tenant that owns the event.
2. Select **Scanner** in the main navigation.
3. If the browser asks for camera access, choose **Allow**.
4. Ask the attendee to open the confirmed ticket from their event registration page and hold its QR code inside the camera frame.

The ticket identifies a registration; it is not permission to check someone in by itself. Evorto still verifies your organizer access, the tenant, the event, and the registration status.
{% figure src="check-in-event-attendees/image-b266ea16e9b15df6.png" caption="Open Scanner from navigation" /%}
{% figure src="check-in-event-attendees/image-e14f8c802a17039a.png" caption="Camera ready to scan a ticket" /%}
### If the camera does not start

- Allow camera access for this site in the browser settings, then select **Try camera again**.
- Close another app that may be using the camera.
- If the device has no usable camera, scan the ticket with a phone's camera and open its Evorto link while signed in as an authorized organizer.
- A visible error is different from an invalid ticket. Do not check someone in until Evorto shows the registration details.

## Verify the registration

After a valid ticket is scanned, check the attendee name, event, registration option, registration status, and any ESNcard notice before confirming. Evorto warns when the ticket belongs to the signed-in organizer, the event is too far in the future, the registration is not confirmed, or it has already been checked in.
{% figure src="check-in-event-attendees/image-5aff3e1c31a71e9c.png" caption="Verify attendee and first arriving guest" /%}
## Check in guests who arrive later

The first confirmation above checks in the attendee and one guest. If another guest arrives later, scan the same ticket again. Evorto shows how many guests are already checked in and how many remain. Select only the number arriving now.
{% figure src="check-in-event-attendees/image-f147d78ff8fc1038.png" caption="Completed check-in and duplicate-scan warning" /%}
{% figure src="check-in-event-attendees/image-a89d3a379a651c3f.png" caption="Organizer checked-in total" /%}
## What completion means

The organizer overview increases by the attendee plus the guests actually checked in. Re-scanning a fully checked-in ticket does not add to the count again; Evorto shows **Already checked in** instead.

Never bypass a warning by changing the URL or using another tenant. Ask a tenant administrator to review your organizer access or the attendee's registration when the displayed details are not correct.

Payload:
Recommendation: use Playwright’s built-in animation settling instead of awaiting `animation.finished`.

- In `tests/support/reporters/documentation-reporter/take-screenshot.ts`, restore the simple two-frame settle and add `animations: 'disabled'` to `page.screenshot(...)`.
- Remove the current unbounded `Promise.allSettled(...)`; an infinite `::view-transition-*` animation can hang it.
- Update the first test in `tests/specs/reporting/reporter-paths.test.ts` to call `takeScreenshot` with a 10-second native view transition plus an unrelated infinite animation. Assert the transition finished and the infinite animation resumed.

Why this is robust: Playwright fast-forwards finite animations and temporarily cancels/replays infinite ones. On this app it cleared 11 active view-transition animations. A synthetic 10-second transition plus infinite animation completed the screenshot in 29 ms, with the transition finished and infinite animation still running afterward.

Relevant setup is `withViewTransitions()` in `src/app/app.config.ts:52` and named transition elements in `src/styles.scss:204`. No files were edited by me.
