   152	
   153	## Reusable knowledge
   154	
   155	- In this repo, `bun run env:runtime` regenerates `.env.dev`, and the package scripts call `dotenv -c dev` internally; direct bare `dotenv -c dev` invocation is the wrong shape. [Task 1]
   156	- `bun run docker:check` is the authoritative runtime preflight and will fail early when required local secrets are missing. [Task 1]
   157	- Authenticated local Browser/Playwright checks depend on the repo's out-of-band Auth0 callback setup; `tests/README.md` points to `APP_HOST_PORT=4200 bun run docker:start` unless the generated worktree port is already registered. [Task 1][Task 2]
   158	- Browser reproduction at `http://localhost:4579/login` showing `Callback URL mismatch` means the callback port is wrong, not necessarily that app logic is broken. [Task 2]
   159	- Browser page evaluation in this environment is read-only for things like cookie mutation; use Playwright fixtures for authenticated state instead of relying on `page.evaluate`. [Task 2]
   160	- For Angular unit tests in this repo, TestBed provider injection is safer than `vi.mock()` for relative imports. [Task 3]
   161	- The generated RPC client's `queryOptions()` type is precise; letting the production wrapper infer the type avoids `exactOptionalPropertyTypes` mismatches. [Task 3]
   162	- A small injectable wrapper around RPC query construction is a clean seam for mocking in Angular TestBed here; `src/app/internal-pages/members-hub/members-hub.component.ts` now uses `MembersHubQueries` with `injectQuery(() => this.queries.hubRoles())`. [Task 3]
   163	- Final local verification that passed here was `bun run test:unit` -> `52 passed (295 tests)` plus `APP_HOST_PORT=4200 bun run test:e2e -- tests/specs/permissions/override.test.ts` -> `8 passed`. [Task 3]
   164	- `git town hack` cleanly created `codex/launch-critical-test-coverage` from detached `origin/main`; the resulting draft PR was `#82` with title `test: harden launch-critical Playwright coverage`. [Task 4]
   165	
   166	## Failures and how to do differently
   167	
   168	- Symptom: direct dotenv invocation fails with `No such option: -c`. Cause: the repo expects package-script or repo-bundled dotenv usage, not a bare shell call. Fix: use `bun run ...` scripts or the repo-documented `node_modules/.bin/dotenv` shape. [Task 1]
   169	- Symptom: Browser auth reproduction fails immediately on login. Cause: the local stack used a worktree-generated callback port not registered in Auth0. Fix: restart with `APP_HOST_PORT=4200` or another registered port before touching app code. [Task 2]
   170	- Symptom: an attempted cookie write in Browser eval fails. Cause: the Browser runtime exposes read-only page evaluation for that operation. Fix: use Playwright fixtures to establish auth state. [Task 2]
   171	- Symptom: Angular unit tests reject the first mocking approach. Cause: `vi.mock()` on relative imports is unsupported by this Angular unit-test builder. Fix: move the dependency behind an injectable provider and mock it with TestBed providers instead. [Task 3]
   172	- Symptom: a hand-written wrapper around `queryOptions()` triggers a TypeScript mismatch. Cause: the restated type is broader than the generated RPC return type. Fix: infer the production type directly and keep the test double structurally simple. [Task 3]
   500	- When cleaning Neon branches, the user asked: "I need you to delete every neon branch that there currently is" and then corrected: "Except for main of course" -> preserve `main` as the only keep target unless the user explicitly says otherwise. [Task 1]
   501	- When an anomalous cleanup is finished, the user followed with: "Then find out how we ended up with 175" -> do root-cause investigation, not just count/reporting, when a surprising branch explosion appears. [Task 2]
   502	- When shipping a mitigation after environment teardown, the user said: "Push this to main, I killes al docker containerx" -> continue with repo-based validation and push flow even if local Docker is already gone. [Task 3]
   503	
   504	## Reusable knowledge
   505	
   506	- For this repo, safe Neon cleanup starts with `neonctl branches list --project-id <project-id> --api-key <api-key> --output json`, because the JSON exposes `default` and `primary`; delete every non-`main` branch after identifying the single keep branch. [Task 1]
   507	- In `evorto`, the relevant Neon project came from `.env.dev.local` and the API key from `.env`; the validated project id in this rollout was `polished-frost-79768881`. [Task 1]
   508	- Branch deletion worked for both `ready` and `archived` branches, and verification should end with `total=1` and only `main` remaining. [Task 1]
   509	- The branch explosion mechanism in this repo is tied to Neon Local, not manual branch naming: `docker-compose.yml`, `helpers/testing/runtime-environment.ts`, `.github/workflows/e2e-baseline.yml`, and `helpers/README.md` all point to a stack that mounts `.neon_local` metadata and uses `PARENT_BRANCH_ID`-driven ephemeral branches. [Task 2]
   510	- Neon operations history is the useful source of truth for branch creation vs deletion patterns; in the validated slice, `create_branch=7` aligned with deleted branch ids and many `delete_timeline` events. [Task 2]
   511	- After deleting stale Neon branches, restart any local containers still pointing at those branches; the local `db` container became unhealthy after its branch `br-damp-base-a9iezwjr` was removed. [Task 2]
   512	- The mitigation that was pushed adds `helpers/testing/set-neon-local-branch-expiration.ts`, which waits for `.neon_local/.branches` metadata, skips when `BRANCH_ID` is already set or `DELETE_BRANCH=false`, and PATCHes `expires_at` on the generated branch. [Task 3]
   513	- The repo change also added `db-expiration` to `docker-compose.yml`, set `NEON_LOCAL_BRANCH_TTL_HOURS: 4` in `.github/workflows/e2e-baseline.yml`, set `NEON_LOCAL_BRANCH_TTL_HOURS: '24'` in `helpers/testing/runtime-environment.ts`, and documented the `db-expiration` + `db-setup` flow in `helpers/README.md`. [Task 3]
   514	- Compose validation in this repo should use the dotenv cascade: `bunx --bun dotenv -c dev -- docker compose config --quiet` passed, while bare `docker compose config` failed because required env like `SECRET` is intentionally unset outside the scripted path. [Task 3]
   515	- The useful validation path here was CLI-only: `bun run lint`, `bun run format:write`, and `bunx tsc` for the helper with `--skipLibCheck`. [Task 3]

   519	
   520	- Symptom: huge Neon branch counts with no obvious manual source. Cause: Neon Local ephemeral branch creation plus incomplete shutdown cleanup. Fix: inspect repo Neon Local wiring, query Neon operations history, and treat `.neon_local/.branches` plus container logs as the fastest proof path. [Task 2]
   521	- Symptom: local database container becomes unhealthy right after branch cleanup. Cause: the running container is still bound to a deleted Neon branch. Fix: restart the local stack after cleanup instead of assuming the container can recover in place. [Task 2]
   522	- Symptom: exact history of all stale Neon branches is hard to reconstruct. Cause: operations history and local metadata show the mechanism but not necessarily the full lifetime of every branch. Fix: confirm the mechanism, then prioritize a durable mitigation such as branch expiration instead of spending excessive time on perfect historical reconstruction. [Task 2]
   523	- Symptom: standalone type-check of the new helper fails with top-level-await/CommonJS issues. Cause: the helper shape was incompatible with the standalone `tsc` invocation. Fix: wrap the logic in `async main()` and terminate with `.catch(...)` before rerunning `bunx tsc --skipLibCheck`. [Task 3]
   524	- Symptom: WebStorm inspection is unavailable. Cause: `/Users/hedde/code/evorto` was not open in that IDE instance. Fix: rely on CLI validation instead of blocking on IDE-only checks. [Task 3]
   525	- Symptom: direct push to `main` succeeds but GitHub reports `Bypassed rule violations for refs/heads/main` and `Required status check "Playwright E2E (functional + docs)" is expected.` Cause: branch protection noise on a PR-oriented workflow. Fix: treat the push as successful but report the warnings explicitly on future similar pushes. [Task 3]
